scope.summary: Enforce evidence-bound bead closure through native br policy plus Required/CI audit, and restore the canonical tracker to a lossless 1092-record state.
out_of_scope: Rewriting legacy close reasons or resolving the 227 advisory-only historical findings.
Treating an uncommitted or self-skipping artifact as close evidence.
Any Wave 1 or Wave 2 implementation outside E5 tracker discipline.

entry_points.name: native br close policy
entry_points.trace: Isolated br 0.2.15 fixture: raw close exits 4 POLICY_VIOLATION, --bypass-policy exits 4 VALIDATION_FAILED, and an exactly evidence-bound close succeeds.

entry_points.name: Required and boundary CI bead-close audit
entry_points.trace: scripts/check_bead_close_evidence.sh exits 0 on the repaired 1092-record tracker with 0 hard findings and 0 required evidence missing; the gate is present in both .github/required/_quality.yml and .github/workflows/ci.yml.

entry_points.name: canonical tracker import and integrity
entry_points.trace: br sync --import-only created 2, updated 9, skipped 1081; SQLite and JSONL both contain 1092 unique records, br dep cycles reports 0, and flush-only plus repeat validation remains green.

entry_points.name: parent-only child closure
entry_points.trace: bead_tracker_guard self-test permits --force only for open parent-child dependencies and rejects an open blocks dependency; the initial guarded E5 close was refused without this explicit classification.

limitations: Native immediate policy applies to br close and rejects status-update closure; br create --status closed is instead caught fail-closed by the post-close Required/CI auditor before merge.

No required-proof/v1 producer artifact was committed for this close, so readiness is honestly recorded as not-ready on scoped-test evidence despite the full workspace fmt, clippy, test, and deny gates passing in the isolated E5 worktree.
The byte-for-byte pre-repair tracker and database backups remain untracked under .doctor/manual-e5-tracker-repair-20260720T104533Z for operator-controlled recovery.
